coaching.coaching: Plan how cross-gate injectivity acceptance criterion gap will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: cross-gate injectivity acceptance criterion gap

coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] complete inventory of existing criterion_prompt_id callers will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: complete inventory of existing criterion_prompt_id callers

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fourth AC (or extend an existing one) that includes the cross-gate injectivity test in its proving command, e.g. `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k 'resolver or injectivity'`, so the injectivity claim is formally verified.
evidence: Testing edge case: 'cross-gate injectivity — no code-review project id collides with any plan-review physical id'
All three ACs use `-k resolver` selector; no AC names or selects the injectivity test
Absence: no proving command for the injectivity claim in the AC block
findings.finding: The Testing section describes a cross-gate injectivity check ('no code-review project id collides with any plan-review physical id') but no Acceptance Criterion captures it, and all three ACs use the `-k resolver` pytest selector — so if the injectivity test is named differently, it is never exercised by any stated proving command.
findings.location: Testing section (edge case bullet) / Acceptance Criteria (all three ACs)
scenarios: An implementer names the injectivity test `test_cross_gate_injectivity`; `-k resolver` never runs it; the AC block is satisfied (all three pass) while the injectivity assertion is never executed.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] No `code-review-*` physical id collides with any `plan-review-*` physical id for any valid criterion id; verified by `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k 'resolver or injectivity'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC for the cross-gate injectivity property (e.g., assert no `code-review-*` physical id equals any `plan-review-*` physical id for the same logical criterion string) to match the injectivity test described in the Testing section.
evidence: Testing: 'cross-gate injectivity — no code-review project id collides with any plan-review physical id'
AC1 covers happy-path resolution; AC2 covers parity/default; AC3 covers unknown gate_key — none covers injectivity.
ABSENCE: no AC references injectivity or collision between namespaces.
findings.finding: The Testing section describes a cross-gate injectivity test ('no code-review project id collides with any plan-review physical id') as a required test case, but no Acceptance Criterion covers it — it is an orphan deliverable with no verifiable AC.
findings.location: Testing section (cross-gate injectivity bullet) vs Acceptance Criteria (AC1–AC3)
scenarios: An implementer writes the injectivity test (because Testing says to) but has no AC to gate on; a reviewer has no criterion to verify it passed; the test could be omitted silently.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC4: 'Cross-gate injectivity: `criterion_prompt_id("F1", gate_key="code_review") != criterion_prompt_id("F1", gate_key="plan_review")`; verified by the same `-k resolver` selector.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the Approach section's caller enumeration to include `eval.py:419` as a third existing caller that needs no change under the default, and note that its `startswith('plan-review-')` guard may need revisiting for code-review eval fixtures in a child ticket.
evidence: Plan text: 'the two existing callers (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:302, src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py:487) need no change'
grep result: src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:419 also calls `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` without any gate_key argument

Plan's Rejected Alternatives section acknowledges eval.py:419 exists: 'Making gate_key required — it would force editing eval.py:419's existing call' — so the plan knows of it but the Approach section omits it from the 'no change needed' list

eval.py:419 guard: `if criterion_id.startswith('plan-review-') ... else criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` — gets the default `plan_review` prefix, which is correct behavior, but the caller is unacknowledged in the authoritative Approach section
findings.finding: The Approach section asserts 'the two existing callers (registry.py:302, criterion_preview.py:487) need no change' — but a grep reveals a third existing caller at `eval.py:419` that also calls `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` without a `gate_key`. The plan is internally aware of eval.py:419 (it appears in Rejected Alternatives) but the Approach section's enumeration is factually incomplete, leaving the caller-count assertion unverified.
findings.location: Approach section — '…the two existing callers (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:302, src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py:487) need no change…'
scenarios: A reader implementing the plan from the Approach section alone would not know to audit eval.py:419 for compatibility — if future children extend the function signature further, they could miss this third caller.

If eval.py is later assigned to a child ticket covering the 'eval-admission' work, the incomplete caller list could cause the child author to overlook that eval.py:419 already hard-codes `plan-review-` as a startswith guard, which may need updating when code-review eval fixtures use a different prefix.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach section, replace 'the two existing callers (registry.py:302, criterion_preview.py:487)' with 'the three existing callers (registry.py:302, criterion_preview.py:487, eval.py:419)' and add a note that eval.py:419's `startswith("plan-review-")` guard is a separate concern tracked by the eval-admission child ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the Approach section to state 'three existing callers' and explicitly list eval.py:419 alongside registry.py:302 and criterion_preview.py:487, noting all three are unaffected by the backward-compatible default.
evidence: Plan says: 'the two existing callers (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:302, src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py:487) need no change'
src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:419 also calls criterion_prompt_id (confirmed by grep)
eval.py:419 is acknowledged only in the Rejected Alternatives section, not in the primary consumer enumeration
findings.finding: The plan claims 'two existing callers' of criterion_prompt_id need no change, but there are three: registry.py:302, criterion_preview.py:487, AND eval.py:419. The third is mentioned only in 'Rejected alternatives', not in the primary consumer enumeration in the Approach section.
findings.location: Approach section — '...so the two existing callers (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:302, src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py:487) need no change...'
scenarios: A child-ticket author reading only the Approach/Scope sections believes there are exactly two callers and misses the eval.py:419 caller when assessing blast radius.
A reviewer counts callers to verify completeness and flags a discrepancy, slowing the review.
findings.suggested_fix: Change '...the two existing callers (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:302, src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py:487) need no change...' to '...the three existing callers (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:302, src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py:487, src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:419) need no change...'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the gate-keying contract (canonical gate names, default rationale, rejected alternatives) in the `ids.py` module docstring or AGENTS.md so the design decision is discoverable without the plan.
evidence: Plan states: 'The .→- namespace rewrite is unchanged, so project.foo + code_review → code-review-project-foo and stays filesystem-safe and injective per the existing charset invariant documented in the module docstring'
Rejected alternatives section documents non-trivial design decisions that exist only in the plan
No mention of updating AGENTS.md, a design doc, or extending the module docstring with the gate-key contract rationale
findings.finding: The gate-keying contract introduced here — that criterion_prompt_id is now gate-aware, that 'plan_review' is the canonical default, and that the rejected alternatives (required param, register_gate) were considered — is documented only in the ephemeral plan text, not in a persistent artifact (module docstring update, AGENTS.md, or ADR). Future contributors adding a third gate or auditing why the default exists will have no discoverable rationale.
scenarios: A third gate (e.g., 'security_review') is added months later by a contributor who doesn't know the register_gate alternative was considered and rejected
An auditor asks why gate_key defaults to 'plan_review' rather than being required
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the `ids.py` module docstring to add a section like: 'Gate-keyed prefixes: criterion_prompt_id accepts gate_key (default: "plan_review") selecting a prefix from _PROMPT_PREFIX. The default is historically correct for existing callers. Adding a gate requires one entry in _PROMPT_PREFIX; making gate_key required was rejected to preserve disjoint-file decomposition across children.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the hardcoded POSIX path `.venv/bin/pytest` in all three AC lines with a platform-agnostic invocation (e.g. `rtk pytest` or `python -m pytest`) so the AC verification commands work on Windows as well as POSIX platforms.
evidence: Plan AC lines literally read: `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k resolver`
On Windows, the virtual-environment binary directory is `Scripts`, not `bin`, so `.venv/bin/pytest` does not exist
The plan provides no note that `rtk` normalizes POSIX paths to Windows paths
findings.finding: The three Acceptance Criteria verification commands hardcode the POSIX path `.venv/bin/pytest`, which does not exist on Windows where the pytest executable lives at `.venv\Scripts\pytest.exe`. If `rtk` passes the path argument directly to the shell without normalization, the commands will fail to resolve the executable on Windows developer workstations or Windows CI runners.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all three `- [ ]` lines, e.g. `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k resolver`
scenarios: Windows platform (developer workstation or CI): running any of the three AC verification commands produces a 'file not found' / 'command not found' error, making the acceptance criterion unverifiable on that platform without manual path correction.
findings.suggested_fix: Change `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k resolver` to `rtk pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k resolver` (or `rtk python -m pytest ...`) in all three AC bullet points, assuming `rtk` resolves `pytest` from the active virtual environment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add one sentence of positive rationale in the Approach section explaining why `gate_key: str` (dict lookup) was chosen over a direct `prefix: str` parameter — e.g. that it mirrors the `gate_key` convention already used in `threshold_for`, `effective_routing`, and `effective_criteria`, keeping all gate-dispatch surfaces consistent.
evidence: Plan states two rejected alternatives (making gate_key required; registering prefix via register_gate) but gives no positive rationale for the chosen mechanism shape over a direct `prefix: str` parameter.

The `gate_key` pattern is already established in `threshold_for(gate:str)`, `effective_routing(gate_key:str)`, `effective_criteria(gate_key:str)` — codebase alignment is the obvious justification but the plan never states it.
ABSENCE: no sentence of the form 'we use gate_key rather than a raw prefix parameter because…'
findings.finding: The plan explains why two named alternatives were rejected but omits a positive rationale for why the `gate_key: str` → dict-lookup design was chosen over the nearest structural alternative (accepting `prefix: str` directly, which would make the function a pure formatter with no internal map). Without this, a reviewer or implementer cannot verify that codebase-alignment with the existing `gate_key` convention was the decisive factor.
scenarios: A future author sees this function and wonders why not `criterion_prompt_id(cid, prefix=PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX)` — the plan gives them no answer.
A code reviewer of the child ticket asks for justification of the internal map vs. a simpler parameter — the plan provides none.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach section, after describing the `_PROMPT_PREFIX` map, add: 'The `gate_key` string (rather than a raw `prefix: str` parameter) mirrors the convention already established in `threshold_for`, `effective_routing`, and `effective_criteria`, keeping all gate-dispatch call sites uniform and preventing callers from supplying an arbitrary prefix that diverges from the gate's registered namespace.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
